title: University Professor
description: University Professors are educators and researchers who teach undergraduate and graduate students, conduct original research, and contribute to their field of expertise through publications and presentations.
content: University professors typically hold a PhD in their field and are experts in their subject matter. They lecture, lead seminars, supervise research projects, and mentor students. They also engage in scholarly activities, such as publishing research papers, attending conferences, and seeking funding for research projects. In the Indian context, professors often play a crucial role in shaping the next generation of leaders and contributing to national development through research and innovation.

financialFeasibility.note: Requires extensive higher education (PhD). While government universities offer subsidized education, private institutions can be expensive. Financial aid and scholarships are available, but competition is high. Salary is generally comfortable but not exceptionally high compared to some private sector jobs.

sectorScore.remarks: Opportunities exist in both government and private universities and colleges. Government positions offer more job security, while private institutions may offer higher salaries in some specialized fields.

relatedCareers.reason: Conducts research and contributes to scientific knowledge.
relatedCareers.title: Research Scientist

relatedCareers.reason: Shares knowledge and educates students at different levels.
relatedCareers.title: Teacher

relatedCareers.reason: Manages academic programs and departments within a university.
relatedCareers.title: Academic Administrator

genderDistribution.note: While traditionally male-dominated, the field is becoming increasingly diverse with more women pursuing academic careers. Some departments may still exhibit gender imbalances.
